desc:
Miles west of the Hollin Road, this thin forest is green and spacious. Thicker
woodlands lie to the north, the dense trees dwarfing the ones on this rocky
slope. Holly grows abundantly, as it does in many areas of Eregion. To the
north-east a ways, the hills rise steeper and less woody. To the south-east, you
can see the riverbed of the Sirannon cutting across the land. Though the hills
are rocky and close to the road, animals still roam leisurely searching for
food.
name: Woody Hills

desc:
On the edge of a large forest that stretches northwards, you look around for
possible danger. As the trees do not grow chokingly thick, you can see that the
forest is quiet and peaceful, though one can never be instantly aware of hidden
danger. Various types of trees grow in the woods, and they are complemented by a
large number of holly bushes and other plants. To the south-east, the Hollin
Road winds alongside the riverbed of the Sirannon as it cuts east-west across
Eregion.
name: Woodlands of Eregion

desc:
Standing in a small clearing, you are surrounded by trees of pine, fir, and
aspen. The ground you are standing on is carpeted by a thick layer of fallen
pine needles and leaves, making travelling here easy on the feet of both mount
and foot traveller. Looking around for landmarks, you spy holly covered hills to
your north-east, and more of this forest continuing to the south. Just to your
east however, there is something strange about the woods. They seem to be hiding
a wood-walled dwelling beneath their thick boughs.
name: Woodlands of Eregion

desc:
The trees in this forest are large and healthy. Adequate water and space make
for an ideal growth environment. The trees in turn provide nourishment and
shelter to the local animal population. Squirrels find the pine cones a tasty
treat, and the deer nibble on the ends of green boughs. On the sides of some of
the larger trees, you see spots where more mature deer have rubbed the bark with
their antlers, trying to rid them of the annual velvet-like covering.
name: Woodlands of Eregion

desc:
Covering the ground almost as a carpet, the bushes of holly grow thick and
plentiful. Dotted with berries of red and purple, the name Hollin seems
appropriate. The name was given to the area by the men of Eriador, but the elves
of Ost-in-Edhil call the land Eregion. Grass attempts to grow in patches where
the holly is lacking, and chunks of broken rock complete the terrain. The air is
clear and cool, with only a slight breeze blowing.
name: Holly Fields

desc:
The moderately flat ground of this area is completely overgrown by holly - both
squat bushes and larger trees. Their red berries stand in stark contrast against
the bright green background of the thick waxy leaves. Many species of birds and
small mammals live amongst the thorny leaves.
name: Holly Fields

desc:
The field you are standing in is almost choked with bushes of holly. As thick as
it grows here, it is no wonder the men of Eriador called the place Hollin. The
bushes grow bright green all year long, as it is designed to withstand very low
temperature. The bushes are dotted with shiny red berries that look like a nice
snack. Birds chirp in happy harmony around you and seem unperturbed by your
presence.
name: Holly Fields

desc:
Under the relatively large overhang, you find the place makes a good windbreak
and heat trap. Nothing but thin moss is growing in this niche of rock, making it
free of the annoying pricks of the holly leaves. There are remains of an old
campfire here, evidence that someone else has used this shelter before. On the
ground you notice sheets of spider webs, as if they go directly down into the
rocks. You wonder why they did not use the walls to build a web. Perhaps it is
their way of conserving heat.
name: Beneath an Overhang

desc:
Deep down inside the hill, away from the lights and sounds you are accustomed
to, the spiders have existed for ages. Feasting on wandering bugs they bide
their time. Waiting for a warm-blooded animal such as yourself to come into the
lair. Bones on the floor are your first clue as evidence of these spiders
tenacity. The next thing you notice, which gives an origin to the bones, is the
various pieces of discarded equipment on the floor. As the beasts have no use
for such devices, they must have come from wandering adventurers like you.
name: Chamber of the Spiders

desc:
As you come to another wall of the cave, you are dismayed to find that the only
exit is back the way you came. Groping through the amalgam of webs and corpses
is not what you wanted to do. In a corner of the room, there is a stack of
corpses, apparently drained of their blood as food for the resident spiders.
Poking at the pile, you feel little resistance. Perhaps the spiders ate the
bones and flesh as well.
name: A Gloomy Cavern

desc:
You hack, push, and flail at the dense tangle of webs. Trying to get through all
of them is indeed a chore. It must have taken dozens of spiders to construct all
of the silky fibers. Among the strands are the now-familiar corpses that are
woven into the webs. Looking like small sacks of flour, they bump into your face
as you move by. Feasting on such things does not appeal to you at all.
name: A Web Filled Cave

desc:
This pit is filled with bugs ans snakes; it must be one of the few dry, out of
the way places around here, as so many things have come in to get out of the
open. Small snakes slither around in the dust, while higher up, spiders of all
sizes have made their webs.
name: A Musty Pit

desc:
As you push through the web covering, darkness embraces you. Even with a light
source, the inky blackness seems overwhelming. Webs brush against your face,
interspersed with soft objects that you assume to be web-enshrouded corpses of
the resident spider's victims. Small clicking sounds can be heard from not too
far away. The smell can only be described as stifling, as the odour is most
unpleasant. By the dim glow of light here, you see the small cavern continuing
to the east.
name: A Dark Pit

desc:
Sloping slightly upward, the wide range of low hills abandons the dense holly
groves. The terrain here is more rocky, and piles of stacked boulders lie here
and there. From the hillside, you have a great view of the vast expanse of
Eregion. Fields of holly lie close at the foot of the hills, while further away
to the west, there are more heavily forested areas. Looking north, you see a
rock overhang jutting out, and at first glimpse it looks like a good temporary
shelter.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
The forests of Eregion grow fertile and thick. Containing a rich diversity of
plants and animal life, the area is in a most stable existence. Trees of pine
and aspen type are the most numerous, but scatterings of fir complement the
place nicely. Vegetation other than tree-type is mostly limited to the familiar
holly bush, though ferns and other ground plants grow in places. Thick tree
boughs shade the territory, making the atmosphere dusky, yet not gloomy. Small
hills arise to the north, where the trees thin considerably.
name: Woodlands of Eregion

desc:
At the base of a small hill range, this forest is roomy and fertile. Trees of
various species flourish in abundance; while lower to the ground, holly grows
thick and strong. The floor of the area is scattered with rocks from the
adjoining hills to your east. Some of the rocks are large, while others are mere
stones. Peering through the forest to the south, you see what looks like logs
stacked up neatly. Resembling a wall of some sorts, you wonder if there is
actually someone living around here.
name: Woodlands of Eregion

desc:
In an apparent area of transition, these small hills join thick forest on the
west, with rolling hills on the east. The trees are spaced apart widely, barely
giving the place shade during the day. Holly grows abundantly around the trees,
its green leaves spattered with the red berries common to it. Animals find this
a nice place to roam, and are seemingly unconcerned with your presence. To the
north lie impassable cliffs jutting upward.
name: Woody Hills

desc:
Not yet able to descend onto the small track down to the east, you look out
across the valley that the trail runs through. Being relatively flat, the valley
is a wetland, yet drains enough to avoid swampiness. Animals are there, taking
in their daily requirement of water before moving along in the endless search
for food. The rocky hills you are in border a large and thick forest to the
west. Through the woods to the northwest, high hills rise up, looking very rocky
and lacking the rich growth found here.
name: Peaceful Hills

desc:
In this section of the hill range, the ground to your east has completely
crumbled, leaving a gentle slope leading down to the track below. Following a
small stream, the trail runs north-south in direction. On other sides, the hills
continue. More of the same geography lies northwards, while on the west, much
larger hills rise up. These larger hills are composed of broken slabs of rock,
and coarse gravel. Passage up the steep west slope is impossible, as an unstable
cliff has formed.
name: A Crumbling Hill

desc:
The hills here are rock strewn and relatively barren when compared to the rich
growth of the forests. So many shards of rock and loose boulders litter the area
that it is easy to get your foot caught in the spaces between them. The rocks
come from above, by way of a steep slope to your west. In times of rain or high
winds, unstable rocks break free and tumble down. Since the rock face is so
unpredictable, you would be wise not to attempt a climb in this direction. Safer
passages of travel lie to your north and south.
name: Hills above a Track

desc:
This slope in the hills faces to the east, catching the morning sun. The holly
on this hillside grows well in these conditions. Boulders and broken rock still
adorn the area, but the resilient holly bushes have managed to crop up wherever
they can. Taking in the view, you see a small stream in the valley below,
winding its way southward towards the Sirannon. A small path follows the stream,
leading to an unknown destination.
name: Holly Covered Hills

desc:
Just to the west is a large cliff, to the north is a range of quiet looking
hills. The hills in the north are covered with thick growing holly bushes, but
not choked like the more dense regions of Eriador. To the south, the old road
continues towards Ost-in-Edhil. Many Elves and Dwarves once used this route in
the days of peace. The riverbed of the Sirannon is slowly vanishing in the
south-east.
name: Bend in the Hollin Road

desc:
Separated from the riverbed here, the road winds northwards a bit, skirting a
large rock formation to your south. Westwards the Redhorn Way continues into the
heart of Eregion, past more holly covered hills and plains. Animals scurry about
in abundance, looking just for the daily food and water they require. Far in the
east you see the towering peaks of the Hithaeglir, tall and forbidding.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
Still skirting the large rock formation, the road bends from north to west here.
The woodlands to the north are rich with wildlife. This land was once trekked
across by many hundreds of Elves and Dwarves, yet little evidence of their
existence remains. The lands seem undisturbed, yet history tells of when Sauron
and his armies razed this place to the ground.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The Road to Moria bends yet again, this time leading either south or east. The
large rock mound is to your southeast, standing tall and unyielding. Just
northwards, you can enter some of Eregion's flat-lands. Holly-filled and
fertile, this area seems unspoiled. Animals engaged in their instinctive
behaviours seem unmoved by your presence. Due mainly to the fact that in
comparison to the days when Elves and Dwarves travelled this route in large
numbers, recent travel has been light.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
Just west of the Hollin Road, your walk takes you through some muddy ground. The
wet ground results from the many small streams flowing at the foot of the
mountains, originating as snow-melt from the tall peaks of the Hithaeglir in the
east. In all directions other than the road, the trees seem to grow together to
form an thick green wall. Westwards the forest continues, and leads toward some
small hills.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
These hills are covered by a dense forest which slow your walk. The trunks and
branches have become tangled over the years, hopelessly difficult to separate
them again. Many strange plants cover the ground, growing particularly tall in
the shadow of the odd walls created by the higher trees.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
Rising above the road to the south, these hills are green and fertile, like most
other parts of Eregion. Passing through the many trees and plants, along with
the many species of animals, it is hard to believe that this land was once laid
to total waste by the dark forces. Looking beyond the road, you see the
semi-deep channel of the riverbed, winding east-west towards far away lands.
Northwards, breakups of the rock have caused the formation of small cliffs.
Though not extremely high, it is too unstable to safely negotiate.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
A long wooden building stretches before you to the east. Rectangular in shape,
the brown log structure looks very old. The mortar in the cracks between logs is
all but vanished. Only the solid construction from which it was made has saved
it from becoming splinters of timber. Examining the logs, you see that they are
remarkably strong despite the ancient design. The forest around you grows thick,
providing a natural camouflage for the ruined lodge. It would take much effort
to detect this place from most anywhere besides right in front of it.
name: Outside a Woodhall

desc:
Thinning here, the trees still grow in large numbers, but more spacious as a
result of the rocky terrain. Large clumps of boulders litter the ground, having
fallen from a slope north-east of here. Looking towards the hills, you see that
they incline gently and are growing thick with bushes of holly. More directly
north lie some flat-lands of Eregion, adorned with holly and an occasional
flower. To the south however, the forest thickens, and you discern a log
dwelling hidden in the trees.
name: Woodlands of Eregion

desc:
The holly grows thick here, its bright green leaves contrasting with the dark
fertile earth it grows in. Piles of rock adorn the area, obviously falling down
the slopes of the hills just east of you. Small spiders crawl over the leaves,
some carrying whole berries as testimony to their huge proportionate strength.
The air is clean here, and the gurgle of a small stream catches your ear.
name: Holly Fields

desc:
Still above the Hollin Road below, you cannot make it directly down because of
the unstable cliffs. In the north, the hills seem to become less steep, perhaps
opening a way for you to get down. Not heavily forested here, the main plant
life seems to be the holly bush, giving more evidence as to why the men of
Eriador called the region "Hollin".
name: Peaceful Hills

desc:
The hills you are in are relatively small compared to the massive peaks of the
Misty Mountains in the east, but are high enough to provide a good view of the
land. Geologic shifts have caused the rocks to break to your east, forming
unstable cliffs. Looking down the cliffs to the east you see the Hollin Road
below and the riverbed of the Sirannon further in the east. Rocks dot the hills,
and holly grows wherever it can. To your north and west are more hills of this
type, with rich woodlands beyond. To the south it is possible to reach the
Hollin Road.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
This particular section of forest has grown incredibly dense. On your east, the
Redhorn Way runs east-west through the area. Originating at the massive west
gate of Moria, it runs westwards toward the ruined city of Ost-in-Edhil, and
beyond that, Tharbad. Around you are more forested hills, except to the north
where a pile of boulders has found a place to accrue. Faint lines are evident in
one of the large rocks, but you cannot find a way to open it no matter how hard
you try.
name: A Thick Clump of Trees

desc:
Just off the Road to Moria, or Hollin Road as it is called, these hills overlook
a rich forest to the north. You can hear birds chirping happily, and larger
animals foraging for food among the woods. Almost directly below you, at the
base of some cliffs, is what appears to be an abandoned wooden dwelling of some
sort. Though not able to traverse the unstable cliffs, the woodlands that lie
further westward look passable. Further south, past the road, is the riverbed of
the Sirannon, leading towards Ost-in-Edhil, the ruined city of the Noldor.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
You stand on a low hill, which has been pierced by the former Sirannon. A
vertical slope leads east down the dry stream. The cliff is not extremely high,
and a few bushes are growing, in its side. To the west the grassy slope of the
hill leads to the Hollin Road, where many merchants were walking four thousands
years ago but which is now deserted and covered by a thick layer of grass and
bushes.
name: Cliff over a Riverbed

desc:
On and on the dried, cracked ground of the shallow bed continues. The absence of
the stream is really the only flaw you can find, as you look around at the
lovely Hollinish landscape. All of the flora greets your eye with vibrant
colour, except for a few dead plants in and around the perished waterway.
name: The River Sirannon

desc:
You are in a wide clearing, at the junction of two paths. Running east- west is
the legendary Hollin Road, traversing the vast expanses of Eregion. The road
leads from the once-great kingdom of Ost-in-Edhil, to the mighty gates of Moria.
The smaller track to the north is of unknown name, but seems to follow a small
stream coming from that direction. The trees at the border of the clearing are
surrounded by holly, a familiar plant of Eregion.
name: A Crossing

desc:
The bridge is made of stone, and is well crafted. Peeling away the moss layer
that covers it, you can hardly detect the intersection of stones. Judging this
to be Dwarven work, you think about the mines of Moria, and the evils that lurk
there now. The purpose of the bridge is to negotiate travel over a small stream
that merges with the River Sirannon. The large river flows just to the south, as
the small stream winds in from the north.
name: A Stone Bridge

desc:
Going east and west here, the track takes you through some muddy ground. The wet
ground results from the meeting of the river and a small stream just west of
here. At the junction, a small bridge of good construction makes the way easily
passable. The larger river being the famed River Sirannon, and the smaller
stream most likely just snow-melt from the high mountain peaks to the east.
Looking up the small stream, you see a rugged track following its course.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
Winding ever-onwards towards the peaks of the Hithaeglir, the road is rough and
rocky. The track continues to the west, leading into more of the same terrain
and eventually to where Ost-in-Edhil once stood. Looking eastwards, you wonder
if this path was used by Galadriel in her flight to Moria. Around you stretches
vast Eregion, fertile and green.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The Hollin Road bends from north to west here, connecting the foothills of the
Misty Mountains to the plains of Eregion. Westward lies a land of holly-covered
hills, while across the shrunken Sirannon to the east, the becomes more severe
as the mountains approach.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
This ancient road bends from east to south here. Westward the land becomes much
gentler as the mountains become more distant. To the north, a deep valley has
been scooped out of the surrounding land - it looks to be almost choked with
vegetation.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
This road was once a monument to the friendship between the dwarves of Moria and
the Noldor of Eregion, but over the millenia since Sauron overran Eriador, the
road has fallen into a state of great disrepair. Even though the dwarves lived
in Khazad-dum for centuries after the elves left Hollin, the west gate of Moria
was kept shut, leaving the quiet lands of Hollin to the beasts and birds.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The Hollin road continues east and west, following the path of the shrunken
Sirannon. The Hollin road is here joined by another equally ancient road,
leading from the empty lands to the north. East of here lies the valley from
whence the Sirannon originates - presumably the Gates of Durin lie at its head.
name: Fork in the Hollin Road

desc:
This ancient road is in great disrepair - it has seen little traffic in the
thousands of years since Sauron drove the Noldor from Eregion. Little remains of
the precisely fitted pavement - the frequent frosts and thaws of the region wave
wreaked havoc with the stone, slowly wearing it away into dust. To the south,
the road meets another ancient thoroughfare.
name: An Ancient Road

desc:
The road is cut here by a swift-flowing stream. On either bank can be seen the
crumbling remnants of a stone bridge that long ago collapsed. The stream is
shallow enough that the debris from the old bridge protrudes from the water,
forming a series of stepping stones. To the south, the stream joins a tiny
trickle of water that flows out of the valley to the east -- judging from the
size of the streambed, something farther up in the valley must have staunched
the flow of water.
name: The Crossing Stones

desc:
The road continues to parallel the Sirannon to the south, leading down from the
mountains to the east out into the hills of Eregion to the west. To the north, a
large ridge rises abruptly, blocking the land to the north from view. To the
west, the remnants of an ancient stone bridge form a series of stepping stones
across a mountain stream that flows in from the north, adding its water to the
trickle of the Sirannon.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The ancient road is dampened by a small waterfall that flows from the top of the
steep slope to the north. The water cascades down in a narrow stream, and then
flows across the remnants of the road into the streambed of the Sirranon to the
south. Eastward, the road parallels the Sirannon, leading farther into the
vee-shaped valley.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The road bends sharply from west to south here, passing between the streambed of
the Sirannon and the steep valley wall. The Sirannon has been reduced to a
trickle - in many places barely enough to dampen the rocks. The valley continues
a good distance to the southeast, and at its head the ancient Gates of Moria
should be found.
name: Sharp Bend in the Hollin Road

desc:
The road follows the course where the Sirannon once flowed. The land rises
steeply to the east, and the road climbs through a series of loops to the level
of the top of the Stair Falls, which once merrily cascaded down a rock cliff to
the southeast. The Sirannon is almost non-existent, so it is safe to walk out to
the foot of the falls.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The Hollin Road to the west descends in a series of loops to the level of the
lower valley floor. To the south, it approaches the area around the top of the
Stair Falls, and the presumably continues onward towards Durin's Gate. The road
here is littered with debris that has washed down from the steep ridge to the
north over the long years, but it is still in fairly good shape after long
centuries of neglect.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The ancient stones of the Hollin Road march into the depths of the dark lake to
the south - the water has risen to cover it completely. A narrow strip of dry
land hugs the northern shore of the lake, nestled between the dark water and the
steep cliffs of the northern edge of the valley. It appears that the dry land
continues for quite awhile - perhaps even to the grey cliffs of the Walls of
Moria.
name: End of the Hollin Road

desc:
Little remains of this ancient road - only occasional stretches where the
climate has spared the pavement indicate that this was once more than a simple
trail through the foothills of the Misty Mountains. To the east flows a babbling
mountain stream, and to the west the land drops sharply into a deep valley - the
slope is too steep to negotiate from here.
name: An Ancient Road

desc:
A stream passes alongside the road to the east, continuing to the south out of
sight. The road is decrepit - often there are great stretches where nothing
except a few flat stones indicates its course. To the southeast, the great peak
of Celebdil dominates the horizon.
name: An Ancient Road

desc:
A stream passes alongside the road to the east, continuing to the south out of
sight; it seems to depart from the road and choose its own path across the land
the the northeast. The road itself does not seem well maintained, or well
travelled; instead it seems abandoned and forgotten.
name: An Ancient Road

desc:
The mere existence of this road is testimony that at one time this area was
inhabited, at least enough to have two places to travel between. However, its
current state seems to deny the idea that anybody dwells here now. The road
continues its lonely journey to the north and south.
name: An Ancient Road

desc:
While barren and rough, with few details to distract the eye, this area does
possess a form of beauty, with its broken stones and untilled soil; some might
see the elegance of the undisturbed lands of Arda, while others see an area with
unlimited potential. The road crossing this place continues to the north and
south.
name: An Ancient Road

desc:
Making an easy bend to the west and south, this road continues its long trek
across these plains. At one time this road may have been paved, and might have
been well kept by careful hands, but now it is little more than a blemish on the
land; remaining visible only because its soil has been packed too much to let
plants take root.
name: A Bend in the Ancient Road

desc:
With no trees near the road to offer shade, the ground is brutally scorched
between dawn and dusk when the skies are clear; pools of water vanish quickly,
and all but the most stubborn grasses wither and die in the heat. It seems
apparent why the land is so abandoned; there is little here for grazing or
farming, so even a simple town would find life here difficult.
name: A Bend in the Ancient Road

desc:
Easily traversing the flat plains that surround it, this road maintains its
simple course from north to south. Winds blow easily through this area, with
little to hamper them; rains fall hard on the ground with few plants to lessen
the blow. Indeed this place shows the damage done by natural forces, in the form
of weathered stone and trenches formed by escaping water.
name: An Ancient Road

desc:
This road is little more than a thin thread passing across vast lands that
remain somewhat barren and uninhabited. Plains stretch out from either side of
the road, while mountain peaks dominate the horizon beyond. The road follows a
simple path to the north and south.
name: An Ancient Road

desc:
Seldom used and even less maintained, this ancient road is little more than an
interruption of the plains and mountains nearby. Alternately covered with a thin
layer of dirt and washed clean by unhindered winds and rain, the surface of this
road is constantly in a state of transition.
name: An Ancient Road

desc:
Continuing easily to the north and south, the road begins to climb into the
mountains to the north. Rivulets have formed in and alongside the road, allowing
rains and melting snow to flow to lower ground, while scoring and eating away at
the road.
name: An Ancient Road

desc:
Loose boulders lie scattered across the road, fallen from the unstable slopes
above. Broken shards of rock have gathered in piles, where they came to rest;
shifting at times as the forces on them change. The road continues to the north
and south between two mountain peaks, ignoring the stones that may eventually
bury it.
name: Road through the Mountains

desc:
The slopes of these hills are loose and rocky; there is little vegetation to
hold the soil in place, so the ground is always shifting and sliding. An old
road passes around from the east to the north through the otherwise
uninterrupted terrain.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
This hill forms the western extreme of a narrow valley that passes just below
it; a small, rubble covered road struggles its way through that valley as it
presses north into the mountains. To the south lies a flat and featureless plain
that follows the road out of sight.
name: The Wide Hill

desc:
As the mountains nearby flatten and spread into these plains, an old road passes
just to the east on its lonely journey from north to south. The road itself has
seen little use in recent years, as these plains begin to engulf and erase it.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
Perhaps once covered by lush, green grasses, or leafy trees planted to offer
shade to those travelling the weathered road to the east, this place is now
barren and lifeless. A dry wind constantly washes over the land, free to wander
in any direction, while all signs that this place was once travelled continue to
fade.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
The only things passing across the dark and hard soil is a forgotten road that
crosses to the east, and the winds that find no leaves to caress or water to
stir.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
Judging by the road to the east, this area was once populated, and supported
enough trade to justify building such a road; perhaps once this land was used
for crops or grazing livestock. All that lies here now is dirt, dust, wind, and
stiff brown grasses which have buried any history that may have once been here.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
Flat and uninhabited plains spread around this place, with little standing
between here and the great mountain ranges to the east and northeast. The soil
is dark and nearly lifeless, too poor to support leafy plants, while a constant
wind sweeps past.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
Small holes are scattered across the ground here, many continuing into dark
tunnels that lead an unknown distance into the ground. Perhaps the only shelter
an animal might have here from predators or the elements, many have collapsed
either partially or completely, but many more seem to be intact.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
Short, dry grasses have managed to grow in this infertile soil despite the harsh
climate and lack of a reliable source of water. Otherwise devoid of vegetation,
the surrounding plains offer little to distract the eye or hold one's attention.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
The ground is damp beneath the heather, as water does not appear to drain from
this bowl scooped out of the foothills. To the west, the land rises to form a
plateau, while to the south the valley continues. East of here is a gully - dry
now, but surely filled with torrents of water after a heavy rain.
name: A Boggy Split

desc:
This deep valley is choked with low vegetation. There seems to be no place for
water to drain - it collects in the valley like a bowl. To the south, the valley
wall can be climbed, leading up to relatively flat land. To the west lies a land
of rocky hills, while north and east the valley continues.
name: Heathery Valley

desc:
You are in a valley with knee high heather. There are no real paths only narrow
trails trodden in by local wild life.You are not sure what could be lurking
below the heather ready to trap you.
name: Heathery Valley

desc:
The gully walls are cut very steep and are made from a crumbly reddish rock - it
would be impossible to climb out from here. To the west, the gully opens into an
area of dense foliage, while to the north the gully narrows considerably,
forming a dark area in which nothing can be seen.
name: Gully

desc:
The rock up above is overhanging so much that it almost forms a cave. The
sunlight can only penetrate a slight north-south crack. There is no other way to
go except the way you came. This would be the perfect spot for an ambush.
name: Valley Cave

desc:
Long distances of plains accented by even more distant mountains lie to the
north, and a road just east of here works its way across the plains to the south
and north. Farther to the east, more mountains arise from the ground, and block
the view of anything beyond.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
As it follows an old roadway that cuts across the area to the east, these plains
are nearly as flat and solid as the surface of that road. The ground has been
stripped away by winds, leaving only patches of prickly grasses to hide an
occasional rodent or reptile.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
An old and weathered road makes a bend to the east and north, circumventing a
small outcropping of rock that protrudes from the ground here. Worn nearly
smooth by the elements, its surface is brittle and loose. By certain stains that
mark it, it could be guessed that birds may find this a suitable perch.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
Entering into the cave, you are met by a blast of foetid air coming from the
back of the cave, which lies straight north. The crevice is fairly large, as you
can stand easily in its main cavern. Noticing the bones of animals small and
large, you are convinced someone or something has made this place its home. The
place is sheltered from the elements, making it a nice dry refuge. The sun
cannot penetrate more than a few meters inside the cave, and thus is always
dark.
name: A Hillside Cave

desc:
This relatively flat plateau sits atop a ridge of rolling hills. The hills
differ from those across the valley to the west, in that they are much rockier.
Thin grass covers the ground here, making it much softer and quieter to walk
over. There is a scattering of trees about, but they are few and far from each
other. The foliage of choice here is the holly bush, along with some hardy moss
clinging to some of the rocks. The table of rock seems to extend deep into a
cave to your north. You lift your nose and detect a bit of foul air from it.
name: A Wide Rock-ledge

desc:
Rocky soil covers the ground here, not wanting to sustain the rich growth like
other parts of the region. The slope is steep here, yet not dangerously so. The
danger comes from the falling rocks around you. Several loose boulders about the
size of a medium Dwarf lie close by. Careening down from the higher slopes
above, the hillside is littered with rock shards. More of the same rocky slopes
continue to the west, but dropping off soon after to form a valley. A short
distance to the east, another valley can be seen among the hills.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
Small groves of trees complement this grassy slope. Looking south you catch a
good view of the Hollin Road, the chief thoroughfare in Eregion. It winds east
and west across the land, and connects Moria to the ruined Noldor city of
Ost-in-Edhil. To the north, the trees and foliage give way to more harsh hills.
Ones adorned with rocks of all sizes, along with steep slopes. Holly grows here
also, as it does in much of the area, dotted with berries of red and purple.
name: A Woody Slope

desc:
Crossing into an area composed of thin woods and rolling hills, you seem to be
in a transition zone. The Hollin Road lies to your south, just over a small
ridge. The east holds some of the same thin woodlands scattered with mossy rock.
At the northern base of this small slope, woods give way to much rockier hills,
ones that appear far more steep as well. If you travel down to your west, you
will be in a green valley with a stream running through it. In places, the
stream backs up to form quiet pools.
name: A Woody Slope

desc:
Clear, shallow, and cool - the stream babbles its way southward toward the River
Sirannon. The junction of the two waterways comes just to the south, past a
small bridge built over the brook. Long ago, the much larger river would have
whisked the smaller current away, towards another river in the west, the
Glanduin. Now however, the little stream seems to dwarf the Sirannon, as the
flow has been dammed up. Northwards towards the origin of the small stream,
green plants and trees cover hillsides and fill orchards. Animal life is
abundant all around, but signs of humanoid creatures are minimal.
name: A Small Stream

desc:
Narrower and rougher than the Hollin Road, this rugged earthen track is running
north-south, parallelling a small stream to the east. It wanders past some holly
covered hills typical of the area, and leads into the heart of Eregion. In the
west a cliff is blocking the way into a hill covered countryside. To the south,
the trail merges with the wider, more frequently used Road to Moria.
name: A Track along a Stream

desc:
Passing by a small gurgling brook, the narrow path you are on runs north-south
here. Its destination is unclear for the moment, but up ahead you see a bend in
the path. Perhaps that will offer you more clues. The stream is located just to
the east of the path, and appears to be only run-off from the tall mountains to
the far east. The stream backs up in places, thanks to the industrious work of
the local beavers. Their dams have created a series of pools along the stretch
of the stream. In the west is a broken cliff.
name: A Track along a Stream

desc:
Dammed up by the work of industrious beavers, the stream forms a relatively
large pool here. Not terribly deep, but too deep to wade, you can swim or boat
easily in the quiet water. Past the dam to your south, the brook continues its
course toward the river in the distance. Green hills and forest still border the
valley, and the path to your west still parallels the water. Small fish swim
around happily in the pond, nipping bugs off the waters surface.
name: A Beaver Pond

desc:
Rising up from the valley eastwards, these hills are rocky and less vegetated
than their counterparts on the western side. The trees are lacking in the
less-fertile soil, and the rock piles are much larger here. Holly still grows,
as it is a hardy plant, able to take the unfavorable conditions. A small stream
flows to your west, continuing down the valley in a north-south direction. The
hills continue to rise eastwards, and you think you spy a cave of sorts to the
north-east.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
Standing on a western facing slope, you overlook a series of beaver ponds in a
lush green valley. The trek to the valley is easy, as the hills slope gently
into it from here. The plentiful water there makes the vegetation thick and
rich, unlike these hills which are rocky and contain little foliage. The hills
are not barren however. Ferns and holly grow around the place in frequent
clumps. To your south are more hills, getting thicker with trees and flora as
you head that way. The rise of the slope stops a bit to your east where it has
formed sort of a rocky plateau.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
Flowing gently over rocks and sand, the little brook winds snake-like through
the valley you are in. Bordered by green hills and forest, the area is full of
life in the form of rabbits, deer, and other local creatures. Looking into the
deeper portions of the water, you see fish swimming peacefully toward a small
pond to your south. The track beside the stream is still following faithfully on
the west side of you. Far in the distance, you see a larger river cutting
east-west across the land.
name: A Small Stream

desc:
The track bends here, still following the small stream on your east. Occasional
mosquito swarms brush against your face, due to the inevitable stagnation of a
few pools of water cut off from the rest of the flow. Otherwise this area is
quiet and peaceful, containing green grass growing almost knee-high in some
places. Stands of woody bushes line the stream in places, but the trail is clear
of these as it follows at a distance. You can continue on the trail by turning
north-east, or you may climb up a small range of hills to your north.
name: A Track along a Stream

desc:
Sloping eastwards here, the hills merge together at a gap where the stream flows
through. Since the slopes of the hills are a bit higher than the running water,
a small waterfall has formed on your east. Running south-westwards from there,
the little brook flows a great distance into Eregion. Alongside this stream, a
narrow, rugged path follows faithfully. Looking down the length of the stream,
you see pools where the flow is being obstructed. You are prevented from going
north by cliffs of unstable rock, but can head south on the small trail.
name: A Track along a Stream

desc:
A small creek of shallow water flows between this rocky terrain. The water is
pretty cold, as it originates as snowmelt from beyond a ridge seen in the north.
The creek is shallow enough to be easily waded by all beasts, and many animals
here are doing just that. Deer, small mammals, and birds all take advantage of
the clear water. Some bathing, others drinking, it makes a pretty sight. The
stream bends to the south-west, and the small path with it.
name: Shallow Water

desc:
From a ridge, water splashes merrily down a small waterfall and forms a stream
westwards. The mountain water looks very clear and fresh, and is cool, almost
chilly to the touch. The ridge is not very tall, but it is steep and slippery,
making it impossible to climb up on it. Westwards, the small stream runs on
through the land of Eregion. A narrow track begins to follow the stream further
west.
name: A Small Waterfall

desc:
Entering into the cave, you are met by a blast of foetid air coming from the
back of the cave, which lies straight north. The crevice is fairly large, as you
can stand easily in its main cavern. Noticing the bones of animals small and
large, you are convinced someone or something has made this place its home. The
place is sheltered from the elements, making it a nice dry refuge. The sun
cannot penetrate more than a few meters inside the cave, and thus is always
dark.
name: A Hillside Cave

desc:
Moving further into the cave, all remnants of sunlight have disappeared. As
something brushes against your face, you hear the flap of wings and small
screeches. Thinking that this is a perfect dwelling for bats, you surmise that
they are sleeping in the daytime, waiting for night to come. A covering of
bat-droppings carpet the floor, making you wonder if this is the source of the
rank air you smelled earlier.
name: A Hillside Cave

desc:
The back of the cave is definitely the source of the rancid smell you detected
earlier. It is caused by numerous corpses of different animals in various stages
of decay. Some have flesh still attached while others are bare to the bone.
Shreds of clothing and rusted equipment dot the floor, testimony of the failure
of others who have tried to conquer this savage beast. It may be out for a
moment, but when it returns will you be another bone on the floor, or will you
succeed in ridding Eregion of this menace?
name: A Foetid Cave

desc:
The narrow strip of dry land along the northern rim of the lake is cut here by a
green and stagnant creek. The creek extends a short way into the northern wall
of the valley, but it's source must be underground, for there is no water
flowing from the high ridges above. The creek is shallow where it enters the
lake - no more than ankle-deep at its edges, so it presents no serious obstacle.
name: Stagnant Creek

desc:
If the water level in the lake to the south and west were just a few inches
higher, this narrow strip of dry land would not exist at all. As it is, the
ground is often soggy, and in many places debris from the deteriorating cliffs
of the northern edge of the valley almost blocks it. The Walls of Moria are
quite close - their smooth, featureless cliffs clearly visible throughout the
day.
name: A Narrow Stretch of Dry Land

desc:
From the crest of this ridge, there is a clear view to the south of a dark lake
that fills the head of a valley. There is no trail leading down to the lake -
the south side of the ridge is much to steep - but there does appear to be a
narrow strip of land along the northeastern shore. To the east, the ridge climbs
to meet a wide shelf that sits above the western end of the valley below.
name: Ridge above a Stagnant Lake

desc:
To the north is a steep slope that descends to a landscape of rocky hills. The
ridge continues to the west, descending rapidly as it goes. To the southeast,
ridge meets a flat shelf that sits atop a large grey cliff that marks the end of
the valley to the south. At the foot of the ridge, a small strip of land along
the northern fringe of lake can be made out, but the descent would be too
perilous to attempt.
name: High Ridge

desc:
This ridge forms the northern edge of the valley where the Sirannon once flowed.
Directly below can be seen the old Hollin Road that leads from Durin's Gate
westward into the holly-covered hills of Hollin. To the north the land is very
rugged, but there is an exposed rock formation in that direction that should
allow you to survey the terrain in that direction. To the west, the ridge begins
to be cut by many crevices, and much of the rock seems unstable, so it is
impossible to proceed that way.
name: High Ridge

desc:
The water is dark and has an evil presence about it. Ripples occasionally
disturb the lakes stillness and puts shivers up your back. The whole area just
smells of evil. The grey stone cliffs extend skyward to your east and the
mountain casts cold shadows everywhere. You are veiled by the eerie shadow of
Celebdil.
name: The Festering Lake

desc:
To the west lies the dam at the head of the valley that has caused this lake to
form. The water is deep here and extremely dark, hiding whatever may be lurking
in its depths. The water smells as well, as if the floor of the lake were
covered in rotting matter that has contaminated the water with the odour of
decay.
name: The Festering Lake

desc:
Directly to the east is a low cliff, some five fathoms high. The top is jagged
and broken, and through a wide cleft at the top a small trickle of water flows,
dampening the rocks. Clearly, this was at some time a large and magnificent
cascade, but now the flow has been diminished to almost nothing. A series of
stone steps have been cut into the cliff, allowing one to climb to the top.
name: Base of Stairfalls

desc:
The Sirannon once babbled forcefully through this channel, but now the streambed
is little more than a rocky trail with a trickle of water flowing from the
higher portions of the valley. Occasionally enough water gathers in a single
place to form stagnant pools, but these are little more than breeding places for
variegated species of algae.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
You are on the side of a mountain with a few sparse plants. To the north you can
climb down into a valley in which the vegetation seems to have taken root. To
the south you can climb up to a mountain trail.
name: Decrepit Mountain Trail

desc:
The hills here are almost barren, with little vegetation around to conceal the
reddish hue of the rocks. The land rises sharply to the south, forming a high
ridge, while in every other direction the hills continue. Farther east, the
Misty Mountains begin, and the triumvirate of Caradhras, Celebdil, and Fanuidhol
tower above the surrounding peaks.
name: Rugged Hills

desc:
To the south is a sharp and high ridge, while in every other direction the same
reddish and dry hills extend. The hillsides are often strewn with boulders and
cut with deep gullies that make travel difficult. To the northwest, the land
becomes slightly more gentle - giving way to a sparse forest of pines.
name: Rugged Hills

desc:
The hills end almost abruptly, coming to a halt at a steep slope overlooking a
valley to the south. There is a road - apparently an ancient one - running
alongside a streambed upward toward the head of the valley among the hills to
the east. To the northwest, a few gnarled pine trees have managed to take root,
and they form the outskirts of a sparse forest.
name: Hills above the Hollin Road

desc:
The small stream that flows here gathers speed and runs down a boulder-strewn
slope to the south, where it forms a small waterfall and tumbles onto the
ancient road to the south. There isn't much soil here, but a few holly bushes
and rhododendrons have managed to survive. To the west, the hills subside and
the landscape becomes gentler, while to the north a sparse forest of pines
provides a little shelter from the chilly winds.
name: Hills above the Hollin Road

desc:
The hills here are more gentle than those to the east, and in general the land
here and farther west seems to be well-watered, in sharp contrast to the dry and
jagged slopes closer to the mountains in the east. To the west, a fair-sized
stream is flowing, while to the south an ancient road passes, running parallel
to an almost empty streambed.
name: Hills above the Hollin Road

desc:
This small but swift stream flows to the south, through a maze of boulders.
There is a high cliff to the east, while the land is fairly flat to the west,
and there appears to be a paved road a short distance away in that direction. To
the north, scraggly pine and fir trees form a thin forest on the eastern bank.
name: The Dark Stream

desc:
The river trickles over the large rocks, creating a good habitat for mosses and
lichen. The flow of the stream is not even close to what it was in years past,
now bearing only a trickle of muddy water. When the river was at its fullest,
only the stout of heart would have attempted to negotiate it. Now though, you
can safely walk over the damp channel. The Misty Mountains can be seen in the
east reaching far into the north. To the west are the lower, warmer lands of
Eregion.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
You can still see the tide marks from when the Sirannon used to roar down this
valley in its full might, but now it barely trickles downstream. The evil work
of Sauron has dammed up the stream, pinching off the life giving substance to
the lands in the west. The riverbed heads west, leaving the evilness of Moria
far behind. There seems to be some sort of track to the north.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
The Sirannon is joined here by a swift-flowing stream from the north. To the
east, barely a trickle of water is draining from the valley leading to the gates
of Khazad-dum. The old road leading to Durin's Gate lies just to the north,
following the streambed farther up into the valley.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
This small trickle of water twists its way down the centre of the streambed. The
stream once carried much more water, but apparently something upstream has
stopped much of the flow. To the west, another stream flows in from the north,
and beyond lie the holly-covered lands of Eregion. On the southern side of the
streambed, a narrow trail - probably made by animals - leads up the steep valley
side.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
The small rivulet that was once the Sirannon is strengthened here by water
flowing in from the north. The rocks of the streambed are damp and slick with
algae, so walking tends to be a chore. To the north, the Hollin Road runs
parallel to the stream, climbing steadily eastward into the higher land at the
head of the valley.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
The foot of this valley lies a bit to the north, and in that direction the
sounds of a mountain stream can be heard. The head of the valley is at a fairly
high elevation, nestled amongst a spur of the Misty Mountains. East and west,
the valley walls are too steep to climb.
name: Sheltered Valley

desc:
The bed of the Sirannon turns to the north here, bounded to the south and east
by sheer cliffs. To the northwest, the ancient Hollin Road can be seen -
paralleling the Sirannon as they both wind away westward into the hills of
Eregion.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
The streambed now takes a strong westward turn. The slope of the bed steepens
slightly as the water trickles down to warmer lands. Far to your east you can
see the ruins of an old building. To your north you can see far enough up the
bank to see part of the broken path. The stream no longer carries enough water
to require a boat, only enough to dampen your feet as you trod along.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
The riverbed of the Sirannon is now heading westwards after a smooth curve in
the north-east towards a bend in the far west. The water used to gurgle along
swiftly, but no longer praises the green land. The area seems especially
attractive when compared to the cold mountains in the east. From the forest land
around you, birds and other small animals can be seen and heard, busily going
about their daily search for food.
name: Riverbed of the Sirannon

desc:
A little west of here, the riverbed joins a smaller river coming from the north.
The water seems impatient at the meeting, as most of its own water has ceased to
flow. Any new addition would surely be welcomed. It is not deep enough here for
fish to swim, but the higher waterlines on the rocks tell you that it once was a
swift flowing volume of water. At least these surroundings seem more friendly
than the mountains to the east.
name: Riverbed of the Sirannon

desc:
You are on the western-most point of a small ridge. The view is nice form this
point, as you have little to obstruct it. The familiar holly bushes dot the
area, along with trees of various types. To the east, a small trail wanders
further along the ridge-top. Looking downslope to the west, fields of holly and
scrub trees catch your eye. Since the slope is not great, you feel certain you
can negotiate your way down. A slab of rock has broken off to the south, forming
a small cliff, and going in this direction would be unsafe.
name: Path along a Ridge

desc:
Walking the small path along the top of the crest, you catch the upslope winds
from both directions. The ridge you are walking is not of great height, just
enough to offer a fine view of the surrounding lands and give you a bit of
security. The vegetation here is abundant, as is the wildlife. A semi-deep
depression in the rocks has caught a smattering of rainfall, and formed a small
pool of drinkable water.
name: Path along a Ridge

desc:
The hills you have been walking in have formed a walkable ridge here. The top of
this ridge is graced by a two-foot wide path that leads east-west in direction.
Though not overly high, this ridge affords a great view in all directions. To
the north lies the meeting of the River Bruinen and Mitheithel, and the river
valley stretching all the way to Imladris. Eastwards holds the mighty mountains,
the Hithaeglir, and inside them - Moria. The south and west regions are the vast
lands of Eregion.
name: Path along a Ridge

desc:
Rising up before a small valley to the south, this green and fertile hill range
is a scenic spot. Growing around you is a multitude of plants and trees. Most of
the plants are holly derivatives, but you can see where ferns and other foliage
has made an appearance. Winding through the valley are two prominent features.
One, a small stream, snakes east-south while running alongside the second
feature, a track of earth and grass. To the south, you can directly climb down
to this path.
name: Holly Covered Hills

desc:
You are high up in the Misty Mountains and the view is still dominated by the
mountains Caradhras and Celedbil. From this spot you cannot see the tops of
these mountains but you can be sure enough that there is permanent snow cover
not far above you.
name: Decrepit Mountain Trail

desc:
The wind buffets you about and the only place that might allow shelter is a
narrow crevice cut deep into the north face of the mountain. To the east you can
see many of the Misty Mountain Peaks.
name: Decrepit Mountain Trail

desc:
The trail changes direction here to negotiate a large spur that protrudes from
the mountain side. The spur climbs high up the back of Celebdil before
disapearing into the clouds. Little knolls and boulders are causing you problems
on the ground.
name: Decrepit Mountain Trail

desc:
This stream flows to the south, curving slightly to avoid the end of a high
ridge that lies to the southeast. To the east a sparse forest of pines and firs
grows in the rocky soil, while to the west an ancient road of some sort can be
made out.
name: The Dark Stream

desc:
The stream flowing just to the west provides enough water to support a thin
evergreen forest. To the northeast, the land becomes much drier and rockier, and
the vegetation quickly peters out. North of here the forest continues, while to
the south the land rises sharply to form a steep arrete.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
The rocky soil provides a home for a few scraggly pines and firs. To the south
and east the land rises steeply, while the thin forest continues north and west.
The trees are ancient, and their trunks have been twisted into strange gnarled
shapes. A small trickle of water flows in from the east - its soft murmur almost
inaudible - and then turns to the south.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
A few hardy pines have managed to take root in the rocky soil. The ground is
still littered with large hunks of reddish rock that appears to have washed down
from the rugged landscape to the northeast. A small rivulet of water winds out
of the hills to the east, and flows almost unnoticed through the rocks until it
drains away to the west. To the northwest, the thin forest continues.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
The landscape is dominated by rugged hills that are almost bare of vegetation.
The land is extremely folded, and often cut by deep channels that have been
carved by the rapid runoff of whatever rain falls here. Tucked among the hills -
confined to a small channel - a tiny stream winds its way from the lands to the
north, almost unnoticed as it flows to the southeast.
name: Jagged Hills

desc:
A tiny rivulet of water flows in from the north - winding its way among the
boulders that litter the slopes of these hills. It eventually finds a course
leading westward. Other than that tiny bit of moisture, the hills are dry and
almost barren. For the most part any soil has been blown away by the strong
winds that blow from the mountains to the east, and only the scoured red rock
remains.
name: Jagged Hills

desc:
The landscape is covered with sharp boulders that may washed down from the
surrounding hills during a flash flood. The faint sound of trickling water can
be made out, as there is a small rivulet winding among the reddish rocks -
barely enough to moisten one's lips. The source of the water appears to be
somewhere in the hills to the north, and it flows onward to the south.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
This rocky plain is littered with reddish-tinged boulders. Little vegetation
survives here, as whatever precipitation falls here seems to quickly drain into
the valleys to the northwest. North of here lie some low mountains, little more
than diminuitive copies of the high peaks of the Misty Mountains to the east.
name: Boulder-strewn Plain

desc:
The land on this plateau is littered with reddish boulders, with very little
vegetation having gained a foothold. The area seems quite dry - whatever rain
falls here must quickly drain away from the rocky hillsides.
name: Rocky Landscape

desc:
The fierce winds that often blow here on the edge of the Misty Mountains have
wreaked havoc with the trees of this thin forest. The evergreen trees have been
twisted into strange shapes - their gnarled trunks assuming a variety of
tortured poses.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
For no apparent reason, the twisted pines of the surrounding forest have
suddenly given way to a circular clearing of packed earth. To the north and
west, the sound of flowing water can be heard. In the centre of the clearing is
a large boulder that brings to mind a pedestal set in the centre of an
amphitheatre. The tracks of many animals can be seen in the earth.
name: A Forest Clearing

desc:
As this stream winds out of the mountains to the east, it passes between a
sparse forest to the east and an ancient road to the west. The water is icy
cold, and even though the stream is shallow, the current is strong enough to
cause problems for those fording it.
name: The Dark Stream

desc:
The smooth stream that flows in from the northeast makes a bend to the south
here, as it encounters an old roadway to the west. The road is rather vacant,
and seldom maintained; spanning the distance between forgotten places out of
sight to the north and south.
name: The Dark Stream

desc:
Curving smoothly from the north to the west, this stream curves around a small
formation of stone to the northwest that has proven too hard to erode by the
constant flow of water. A narrow beach follows the eastern shore of the stream,
and small pools of still water filled with trapped debris have formed along the
waterline.
name: The Dark Stream

desc:
Occasionally washed clean of flotsam when the stream to the west floods, this
place tends to accumuate whatever happens to fall into the water upstream. The
water makes a curve nearby that creates various eddies and pools, which gather
branches and floating weeds; the smell of composting leaves a bitter fragrance
in the air. A steep bluff rises to the east, marking the edge of a family of
mountains.
name: Along the Stream

desc:
From these low peaks, which mark the southwestern corner of a small group of
hills, a short stretch of a a stream lies within view to the west. A small plain
extends to the south, and rising mountains dominate the view to the east.
name: The Low Hills

desc:
These low mountains are covered by a thin blanket of tough grasses, which seem
fairly healthy by their green hue. The grass becomes more dense to the east, as
the ground descends into a small valley. Plains lie to the south and southwest,
while mountains reach to the north.
name: The Low Mountains

desc:
Large tufts of thick, green grasses grow scattered around a large mountain
spring that has formed here. No trees stand here, perhaps hindered by the loose
soil, but an abundance of small flowering plants, grasses, and reeds have
sprouted and survived. The waters of the spring flow to the south.
name: A Mountain Spring

desc:
Some small and unhealthy trees have managed to grow here, taking what little
water that they can and trying to hold their roots and leaves as the wind tries
to blow them over. The source of their water is a quiet stream to the east,
whose water is dark and swift.
name: Among the Trees

desc:
Curving gently to the south from the east, the current of this swift stream
slows a bit as the ground flattens and the water spreads into a wider, more
shallow channel. Taking advantage of the calm water, some reeds have taken root
and offer a little colour to the otherwise bland landscape.
name: The Dark Stream

desc:
Channeling down into a narrow and steeply banked streambed, where at one time
the flowing water found a soft spot in the underlying sediment, the dark current
moves silently south. A small clearing extends to the east, marked by some old
and twisted trees, but the western bank is too steep to climb.
name: The Dark Stream

desc:
The distant mountain peaks to the north and east are all that hinders sight or
the winds in this area; the bleak plains that lie between offer no shelter or
obstacle. Small cyclones of dust stir up from time to time, and dance around
playfully until the winds change and the dust settles back to the ground.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
As the thin layer of silt along the ground is periodically whipped into a frenzy
by gusting winds, and large clouds of dust turn the air into a dark and choking
shroud, this place becomes even more unwelcoming than it had been. With no tall
plants to slow the winds, the land remains barren; and while it is barren, it
will remain uninhabited.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
Flat and somewhat featureless, baked solid by the scorching sun over many years,
the ground is totally bare in places where the wind has swept unrelentingly
across it. Patches of stubborn grasses still cling to the earth, drawing water
that lies sheltered below the shell of hardened soil; sometimes eaten by roaming
animals in need of moisture.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
A faint trail leads from west to east here, but the places that it connects are
not easily distinguished. To the west, a band of colour that contrasts the
darker soils here runs through the plains, while to the east, at some distance,
a twinkle of green and brown catches the eye.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
Heavily laden with sorted dust and silt, the ground along these plains is soft
and giving, at least as far down as the harder clays below. The occasional gust
of wind will stir up bits of sand and hurl them to pelt whomever is standing in
their way.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
Aiding the smooth transition from low plains to the spectacular mountain peaks
on either side, these hills offer a decent view of the surrounding landscape. To
the north and east extends a great mountain range, while to the south and
southeast lie featureless plains. A road passes to the immediate west.
name: Some Low Hills

desc:
As it is worn away with the rest of these mountains, this particular peak's
destruction also takes its toll on the road below. As boulders dislodge and
mudslides move down the western slope, the unkept path is slowly but steadily
buried beneath the earth. Given time, no sign of that road will remain here.
name: A Weathered Peak

desc:
Winds blowing along this mountain area are often cold and dry; small patches of
shaded ground seem to draw away the life and energy of the wind. What remains is
a chill breeze that seems content to make travel harder by filling the air with
choking dust and stripping away moisture to inspire thirst.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
Marking the southern edge of a vast range of mountains, the peaks here are a bit
lower than the ones to the north. Despite the fact that the view to the north is
blocked in this way, the slopes of these mountains offer a magnificent view of
the moon in the night skies to the south, as it travels slowly over wide and
undisturbed plains.
name: Above the Plains

desc:
Thrust upwards by some great cataclysm, or perhaps molded by the very hands of
the gods, large and magnificent mountains rise out of the ground to the north
and gaze out over the flat plains to the south. Winds passing freely over the
otherwise flat land seem troubled by these great obstacles, and whip around as
they are forced to change course.
name: At the Foot of the Mountains

desc:
Inhabited only by wild animals, and marked by occasional tufts of grass that
have managed to survive the elements, these plains are essentially barren and
dead. The soil itself may not be infertile, and might be tilled to raise crops,
but the area is so desolate that perhaps this will never happen.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
This particular patch of land is a bit different from the lifeless plains
nearby, primarily because there is a small dip in the land which has filled with
water. Thick, green grasses blanket the ground around the pond, thinning and
shriveling as they spread away.
name: A Spring in the Plains

desc:
The shadows cast towards the north by the noon sun, or even a bright moon
watching from the south, pale in comparison to the ominous and dominating
mountains to the north which seem to gather shadows to them. Plains reach to the
south and west, to the horizon, while the eastern view is marked by more distant
mountain peaks.
name: Near the Mountains

desc:
Dark and sometimes unstable slopes mark these low mountains; occasional scars
where soft mud or loose gravel shifted and slid are all that break the
continuity of the landscape. No trails or signs of settlement exist here; either
they were long since buried by moving dirt, or never created to begin with.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
Great and rough mountains spread in all directions, blocking the view of any
distant features or obstacles, as well as the sun or moon if possible. This
place has always been somewhat remote and alone; its special feeling of
sanctuary is hardly disturbed by a visitor who is not only mortal, but also
otherwise insignificant.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
These mountains grow steadily larger and more magnificent towards the north,
where they extend out of sight; to the south, they descend into wide and rather
featureless plains. Little sign of animal life can be seen here, and even less
evidence of civilisation exists. To the east and west, the rising mountains
continue.
name: The Rising Peaks

desc:
As the landscape makes its slow transition between the plains to the southwest
and the mountains to the northeast, a narrow and dry streambed meanders toward
lower ground. While sometimes found to carry water, during the thawing of the
snows from the mountain peaks, it is now dry and empty.
name: Edge of the Plains

desc:
Carved from the soft soil of the plains, as water lifted the lighter bits of
dirt and sand and carried them with it, this place is now left empty and dry.
The walls, too hard to be dissolved by the moving water, seem stable and secure;
only when waters fill this small pit is it unsafe.
name: A Small, Dark Cavern

desc:
This place is completely surrounded by vast distances of plains, with mountains
to the north and east, and at some great distance to the south. Little can be
seen to the west, as the plains roll out of sight, except for an occasional
glimmer of green and brown against the stark skyline.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
Far off to the east, a thin stream of water makes its way through a gap between
the mountains and collects into a wide and dark pool. The mountains that the
water divides spread like sweeping arms in an arc to the north and south, some
distance away. These plains extend out of sight to the west.
name: The Barren Plains

desc:
These hills fare the worst in all kinds of weather. The sun, when overhead, will
scorch the ground and bake the soil. Newly fallen snow will fall between stones
and wedge them apart as it refreezes. Rain strikes the rocks as it falls
unhindered, then washes gouges into the mountain slopes as the water flees
downhill. Even the winds push stone against stone, as natural forces work to
change the terrain.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
Far beyond the view or influence of any civilisation, surrounded only by more
mountains, barren of life, devoid of vegetation, uninviting, and impersonal are
these ancient mountains. Older than life, more weathered than any gnarled tree,
and as lonely as any widower, these mountains continue to watch the rising of
the sun and the setting of the moon as they have for many mortal lifetimes.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
The ground throughout these mountains is rather dark, when not capped by a
blanket of snow. Thick soil covers the slopes of the mountains, washed down by
rains and wind, while the peaks themselves are exposed rock and weathered
boulders. The mountains extend in all directions.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
Low grasses have sprouted at the bottom of this slope, sheltered from falling
debris by a large formation of rock standing in the path of sliding dirt.
Mountains seem to spread out in all directions, each blocking the view of what
lies just beyond.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
Nestled and hidden in the mountains, a small clear spring of water has formed,
as water trickles down the mossy rocks of this slope. Well shaded by the looming
peaks overhead, the water has collected in narrow pools, each accented by mosses
or mushrooms.
name: A Mountain Spring

desc:
This mountain is encircled by countless others that roll across the land to each
horizon. Travel through this area can be dangerous, as loose rocks and dirt tend
to shift unexpectedly, sometimes causing an even greater slide of the nearby
slope.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
Sharp stones jut out of the sides of these hills, hanging dangerously onto the
unstable slopes. Exposed by a constant battering of the elements, these rocks
have little left to support them, and will likely become part of any rockslide
that takes place.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
Large piles of fallen rock and mud accumulate at the base of these hills, either
too massive to be carried away by flowing water, or allowed no exit to lower
lands. Passage between peaks is thus a difficult affair, as the loose gravel and
dirt is prone to shift under any additional weight.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
Long scars run down the side of this hill, as flowing water, either from heavy
rains or melting snow, cut its way through the loosely packed soil as it moved
downhill. In many places along these marks large areas of the slope have
shifted, as the weight of the water soaking into the dirt became too much for
the slope to bear.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
This flooded plain is very wide and somewhat musty, but the water is shallow
enough to be easily crossed. This ford continues to the south and east, as it
loosely follows the path of the river that created it; mountains and plains
dominate the surrounding area.
name: The Flooded Plain

desc:
Hardly a hospitable place, with hard, rocky soil, no shelter from the sun or
winds, much less the occasional rain, and unsettled by any, these mountains do
not even offer a vantage point or tactical advantage. So they remain as they
always have: unused, unwanted, and undisturbed.
name: The Weathered Hills

desc:
The various peaks of hills, all different in height and breadth, are scattered
in all directions across the terrain. The soil is rocky and rough, as the hills
themselves are slowly weathered down by winds and rain; few if any plants have
managed to take root in the poor dirt.
name: The Weathered Hills

desc:
Rising above a narrow beach to the west as a steep cliff face, down which an
occasional rock is prone to fall, this mount looks down on a stream that flows
by silently below to the north and west. Other mountains reach off to the south
and east, while the distant north is flat and plain.
name: Above the Cliff

desc:
Overhung with the sparse or dead branches of some knarled trees, with a dark
stream passing to the north and west, this place is the closest this area has to
a relaxing beach. The trees have grown close to the water, so at times when the
water has risen, the soil around their roots has washed away, leaving some of
them perched above the ground in an eerie fashion.
name: The Small Beach

desc:
Gurgling quietly as it encounters a barrier to the south, the stream makes an
abrupt turn from the north to the west. A small, clear beach has formed to the
south, with some small trees clinging to life along the water's edge. The beach
also extends to the east, following this curve in the stream.
name: The Dark Stream

desc:
Curving from the east to the south, following some unseen features of the
surrounding terrain, this quiet stream scarcely makes any sound of its own.
There are no thundering waterfalls, nor turbulent rapids, nor babbling shallows
along the course of the water; it seems content for now to simply drift.
name: The Dark Stream

desc:
The water of this stream runs cool and slow, but it is somewhat deep. Flowing
with the curvature of the land to the southwest, the water is cloudy with silt
and mud which is blown into the water from the surrounding plains. Aside from
the sound of water lapping against stones, and the wind drifting across the
area, this place can be rather quiet.
name: The Dark Stream

desc:
As it drains the flooded land to the northeast, this stream runs deep and
narrow, as though forged in what was once a natural dam. The water itself
continues downstream to the west, making almost no sound as it moves.
name: The Dark Stream

desc:
The land here is flat, and broad, and covered almost completely by a shallow
water. The water, which seems to enter this place from the northeast at some
distance, is cloudy from the soil that is blown into the air nearby, yet it is
refreshed and washed away before it can become muddy.
name: The Flooded Plain

desc:
The higher walls of this and other nearby hills have helped to trap water in a
small, flat valley below, where it flows slow and shallow to the north. This
feature of the land would make this an excellent ford of the river, if indeed
crossing it elsewhere became difficult.
name: Above the Pool

desc:
Slopes made slightly unstable by the running water that eats away at its base to
the north, this low mountain affords a view over the terrain to the north that
reveals it as being rather featureless and barren. Mountains rise and extend to
the south, and a large, dull pool of water lies to the northeast.
name: Above the Stream

desc:
Shaded at most times of the day by the taller mountains to the south and east,
this small beach boasts several healthy clumps of grass. With a stream passing
to the west to supply water, and some abundance of grass growing in patches
here, it is possible that some wildlife exists in this area.
name: A Small, Grassy Plain

desc:
Here the bit of water from the Sirannon joins a smaller river on its long bend
towards the south. The water seems to rejoice at the thought of more company
along the way. The stream coming from the north is probably no more than some
snowmelt from the mountains, but even so it dwarfs the amount currently flowing
in the riverbed. The river must have been dammed upstream, as there is hardly a
trickle in the streambed that should be carrying a decent volume.
name: Junction in the River

desc:
These softly rolling hills provide a good view of the surrounding landscape. To
the west, jagged hills dominate the view. Fertile hills, crowned with trees, can
be seen to the south. The woods grow more dense to the east, where the trees
entirely cover the multitude of hillocks.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
After walking past the road through a rather flat area, you find yourself on the
top of a small cliff. You look down from the top into the riverbed of the
Sirannon. The river is dry now, but the former stream has dug a cliff in the
soft ground, not more fifteen feet high. The sheer cliff rises to the west of
the riverbed.
name: Cliff over a Riverbed

desc:
Directly to the east, a dam of large boulders and tree trunks has been
constructing - completely choking the flow of the Sirannon. Only a few rivulets
force their way through the barrier, and they quickly merge and flow over the
Stair Falls, which have been reduced to barely a trickle. The shallow valley
beyond the dam is now home to a dark, still lake of stagnant water, which
seemingly extends all the way to the Walls of Moria at the head of the valley.
To the north, the ground is too rough to traverse, but the old Hollin Road can
be made out as it wends its way up to this height.
name: Top of the Stairfalls

desc:
The foothills here roll along a deliberate march toward some high mountain peaks
to the north and east. To the south, the hills can be seen receding in a
spectacular vista that extends as far as the eye can see. Sparse vegetation
grows among the rocky crags of the the hills between large boulders.
name: Among the Hills

desc:
Little more than a footpath, an old trail runs west from the roadway, and
continues on under the protection of a high, curving cliff. This area might once
have served as a campsite for travellers journeying through the pass. To the
north and west, the cliff towers above the camp, but to the south the vista
opens up.
name: Trail off the Road

desc:
The old road here cuts through the mountains. It is very weathered and, given
enough time, will eventually erode away into nothing. The path of the southerly
road is straight and deliberate, as it extends out of sight. High mountains
