border the road to the east while some hills can be seen to the west.
name: Old Road through the Mountains

desc:
Turning eastward, the road from the south approaches a narrow pass through the
mountains. To the south it stretches onward as far as the eye can see. Though
cracked and pitted in a few places, the surface of the road is in surprisingly
good condition, having been created simply by flattening the bedrock itself.
name: Old Road through the Mountains

desc:
The road continues east and west. Its journey takes it through a gorge that
grows more narrow to the east. Westward the pass opens out somewhat, at the same
time losing a little altitude, and more vegetation can be seen growing to either
side of the road in that direction.
name: Old Road

desc:
A few low bushes sprout up protecting this narrow pass, but lacking sufficient
topsoil to sustain their roots, none is thriving. The central part of the pass
has been levelled to create a road through the mountains. The steep cliffs
looming to either side offer the traveller little choice but to follow the pass
onward through the mountains.
name: Old Road

desc:
Turning from north to west, the road is forced along the path dictated by the
pass through which it travels. The road is in remarkably good shape, but
countless boulders are littered across its somewhat cracked surface. Though it
is still a good thoroughfare for travellers on foot or even horseback, it would
no longer afford access to any large cart or wagon.
name: Turning Road through the Mountains

desc:
The road runs from north to south here, constrained by the narrow pass through
which it journeys. This is the narrowest section of the gorge, with tall cliffs
looming above from both sides, and even at the height of noon, deep shadows
exist here. Many large boulders lie all across the road, having fallen at some
time from the cliffs above. A chill wind whistles by almost constantly.
name: Narrow Mountain Road

desc:
The road here is, for the most part, intact. The red stone that comprises its
surface lies smooth, as if untouched by time. Traversing a narrow pass through a
mountain spur, the road is somewhat protected from the elements. The narrow
canyon walls serve to guide the road along its path at the foot of the looming
peaks.
name: Road through the Mountains

desc:
Cresting a final rise, the road from the plains reaches the mouth of a mountain
pass. Turning to the east, the ancient thoroughfare starts its journey between
the towering peaks of the mountain spur that cuts from east to west across the
land. In the comparative shelter of the pass, the roadway here is significantly
less decayed than might be expected in a land that has so long been unoccupied.
name: Road through the Mountains

desc:
An occasional blast of cold air rushes up the side of this mountain, as the
winds of the chasm to the north encounter the sloping ground. Aside from the
sounds of a loosened stone or dried leaf rolling along the ground, the low rush
of moving air is all that can be heard.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
A large chasm cuts across the land to the north; more of a valley between high
peaks, it effectively parts this region from the areas across it. A quiet and
chill wind carries through the valley, giving the whole place a somewhat cold
and unwelcoming feel.
name: A Mountain View

desc:
Cold, lifeless, distant from settled lands, and at times hostile, these tall
mountain peaks are poorly suited for travel or for rest. Sharp stones lie
scattered around the soil. No trees grow to provide firewood, no water flows or
even sits stagnant to soothe wounds or refresh the tired wanderer.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
A stunning view of mountainous terrain, and little else, is offered here. In all
directions, mountains extend to the horizon and block sight of anything beyond,
if indeed anything lies there. Perhaps this place is all that now exists; no
reminders of comfortable dwellings or sustaining food appear on the steep slopes
of the mountains. This place is forgotten by the world, and indeed that world
seems only a lingering memory.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
Great distances separate this place from any safe havens; no shelter exists to
offer protection against the burning sun or freezing snows. The ground is soaked
with each rainstorm, and gravel is thrown through the air when winds blow
through the area; no feature of this place makes it suitable for meaningful
rest.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
To many, a place of total despair is often pictured as a firey pit of anguish in
which the unfortunate are doomed to burn for all of time. However, what is not
often perceived is that a cold, lifeless, distant, and compeletely lonely area
such as these mountains can also take its toll on sanity, and might be just as
torturing.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
An old stone staircase winds its way down from the mountains above. Clear, cold
water has collected at the basin of this high valley, creating a small mountain
lake. With steep slopes to all sides, a lush green alcove has flourished here.
name: Tiny Mountain Vale

desc:
Though not coming close to the heights reached by the loftier Caradhras,
Celebdil and Fanuidhol, these peaks nonetheless reach high into the sky. An old
stone staircase seemingly carved into the mountain can be seen winding a narrow
path down the slope.
name: Mountain Staircase

desc:
Tree-lined peaks soar high above, even from this lofty vantage point. This long
ridge reaches out from the Misty Mountains, like mighty fingers that grasp at
the lower-lying plains beyond. Dipping a little to the east, the slopes draw
closer to a narrow pass.
name: Along a Ridge

desc:
From both north and south mountainsides, an ancient road climbs up to a narrow
pass through this spur. The vantage point here allows seeing any traveller along
the way for a long distance. An eerie quietness pervades this mountain perch,
waiting, watching.
name: Silent Peak

desc:
Buried deep beneath the Misty Mountains, no natural light has ever reached this
tunnel. The rough walls bear no distinctive features, and getting lost within
these caverns would be very easy. But from here the choice of route is rather
limited: the tunnel stretches west and south.
name: Disused Tunnel

desc:
A roughly cut tunnel stands here. Deep in the dark the tunnel curves around
eventually twisting a path to the north. While clearly hewn from the rock, this
passage seems to have not been touched by the hand of a living thing in at least
an age.
name: Tunnel in the Dark

desc:
The large crack to the south acts as a shield, blocking this cave from view. To
the north a hole in the stone leads into another another small cave. A smooth
patch of ground seems to have been cleared along one of the walls of this cave,
while rubble lies on the floor everywhere else.
name: Behind a Crack

desc:
The floor here is jagged, with hundreds of grey rocks creating an uneven
surface. At the far end of the cave a pool of water has accumulated in a basin.
The water seems to be trickle from holes in the rocky walls. It dribbles slowly
down their surfaces until collecting in the clear puddle.
name: Pool of Water in the Rocks

desc:
The cave is tall and wide. In the centre, a great chunk of rock has fallen from
above and rests like a stone table. Above, there is an opening from whence this
boulder must have come from. The walls are studded with twinkling stones
guarding any secret, dark crevice.
name: Cave under the Mountain

desc:
The tunnel worms its way up and down, with walls that are exceedingly worn and
smooth. Tufts of mould grow dotted here and there on the walls. A strange ooze
runs down the tunnel like viscous drool from above. Something dug or eroded this
tunnel long ago, but what it was can only be guessed.
name: Smooth Winding Tunnel

desc:
The cavern walls shine in the dark as if covered in some liquid. Underfoot, a
sticky ooze covers the ground. A horrible smell pervades this cave though the
source is unclear. The air is perfectly still, and silence echoes deafeningly
through the cave.
name: Sticky Natural Cave

desc:
Blackness envelops this room like moonless night. The walls are impossible to
make out, and the floor is buried under something soft and squishy. The
unidentifiable substance underfoot periodically squelches and spurts something
foul onto the ground accompanied by a fetid smell. The air reeks of death.
name: Amongst the Decaying Flesh

desc:
The cavern extends to the east and north; cobwebs spring into view showering the
cave with their white, silky strings. A dreadful skittering can be heard from
somewhere nearby, and a smell which can only be described as insulting to the
very stones lingers here in dreadful delight.
name: Webbed Cavern

desc:
Thick cobwebs span the cave stretching from wall to wall. From up above,
individual glistening strands of webbing coated in a sticky goo drift about on
any breath of air that will carry them. As for the ceiling above, only darkness
can be seen beyond the hanging lines. Darkness... and something else.
name: Hanging Webs

desc:
The floor is strewn with cobwebs and bones. In the south of the cave, a small
platform of rock stands a few inches above the rest of the floor. The stone
platform is heavily webbed and from it hang several bodies. Thousands of eggs
have been laid across the platform, affixed to corpse and stone.
name: Slimy Nest

desc:
Putrid and seeping, a pile of corpses dominates the centre of this grim den. Pus
drips from the gashes inflicted upon the mangled bodies, yet they are completely
devoid of blood. The sockets, where the eyes once rested, are now bare and cold.
A ghostly chill runs through the room as if death itself was watching over it.
name: Heap of Lifeless Corpses

desc:
The rocky tunnel swerves hither and thither snaking its way to the west and
south. Sticky webs suddenly appear as the tunnel leads westward, a few large
bones dangling within the strands. Some debris rubble can be seen to the south
where a large cave-in occurred, making it difficult to pass through.
name: Partly Caved in Passage

desc:
Debris has fallen from above, creating a mound in the centre of this tunnel and
hindering the passage. The natural cavern boasts little in the form of living
things, but the air is not completely foul. A trickle of dirt falling from the
ceiling indicates that the surface lies not far above.
name: Partly Caved in Passage

desc:
Underfoot the floor is predominantly comprised of stone with streaks of dirt
here and there. To the north the blackness only grows deeper in the air carries
a foul scent. Southwards, while no trace of light can be seen, a hint of fresh
air can be felt.
name: Dark Passageway

desc:
A musty scent hangs in the air in much the same way arrows do not. The walls
here are a textured amalgamation of stone and earth. Any sound produced in this
tunnel is instantly muffled by the copious amounts of exposed dirt.
name: Curving Dirt Tunnel

desc:
Thin streams of dirt trickle from the soft earth above. Above a breath of air
can be felt washing over the the area. To the north the darkness of the tunnel
winds away. Underfoot the soil is soft and roots creep about eager to catch
unwary step.
name: Beneath the Loose Earth

desc:
Surrounded completely by tall mountain peaks that dominate the land in all
directions, this place shows no little sign of supporting wildlife, and even
less of civilisation. A flowing breeze which winds its way between the peaks is
all that disturbs the otherwise complete calm.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
Deathly quiet, when rocks are not tumbling down the steep slopes, and when the
winds subside long enough to let the heavy air come to rest is all that envelops
these mountains. No insects buzz through the air, no birds call from overhead.
Only an unsettling peace and stillness remains.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
The ceiling dips slightly, making this cavern a bit shallow. The walls, now the
texture of granite, slide into the floor making the available space to stand
scarce. Nooks and crevices can be seen travelling deep into the rocky walls
causing numerous black holes to peep out.
name: Low Cavern

desc:
The cavern opens up a little to give some standing room. To the east an
extremely cramped passage can be seen disappearing into the rock while to the
north and south two more caves connect with this one. The air is still and
stale; nothing breathes here.
name: Cramped Cave

desc:
The ceiling in this cave hangs rather low to the ground with sharp stalactites
jutting down from it. The air feels close and anxious with and almost deafening
silence. A black strip of sparkling black rock seems to run like a river along
the ceiling.
name: Crumbling Cavewall

desc:
The ceiling of this little space hangs low, making it difficult to pass through
comfortably while the floor slants upward. The limestone walls crumble and
trickle shards of rock onto the ground at the slightest touch. Westwards the
path seems to become quite difficult, while the path east is a bit more open.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
Inside this fissure of rock, shelves of limestone form a scaffolding that might
be possible to scale. Up above it is difficult to see a path forward as
stalactites hang down blocking vision in that direction, but perhaps a small
hole is visible. To the west a very narrow opening can be spotted; maybe it is
big enough to squeeze through.
name: Naturally Formed Passage

desc:
Glistening rivers of obsidian stone writhe their way up the walls, intertwining
throughout the rocky strata. The cave, much like a horrible black mouth, has
stalactites and stalagmites scattered haphazardly throughout the rounded walls,
and extends off to the west and south. Naturally formed steps provide a path to
what appears to be a cave above this one.
name: Black Cavern

desc:
The cavern walls here are composed of a white, speckled stone. One solid grey
rock leans against the northern rockface, and a black slab covers part of the
pavement. Both are in stark contrast to the smooth marble walls. A crack runs
along the ceiling of the passage. Nothing can be seen behind the darkness that
lives in the crack.
name: White Stone Cavern

desc:
Boulders dominate the floor of the tunnel, with large pockets of space clearly
visible in the ceiling from whence they undoubtedly came. The majority of the
fallen stones are piled up in the northeast part of the passage causing the
entire area to be tilted. The stones composition is unclear, but it crumbles
quite easily. The tunnel bends sharply north and east.
name: Partially Caved-in Passage

desc:
A natural cave has formed here. The grey walls are marred with streaks of brown
and black, and the floor is uneven with rocks taking up almost every inch of
space. To the north, east and west it appears there are adjoining caverns, but
it is impossible to make out what is in them from here.
name: Beneath the Mountain

desc:
A natural stair rises up into this rocky dome. The tunnel floor can still be
seen below, dominated by boulders, as it bends to the north and east. The rock
here is of a crumbly nature which makes climbing impossible. A tiny crack at the
top of the dome can be seen leading outside to the west.
name: Rocky Dome

desc:
Smaller than most, this cave is bone-chillingly cold and tight. Its ceiling is
low and very uneven with large, jagged rocks jutting down in many places. The
floor is similarly rugged with scattered boulders and large holes all around. A
gaping fissure in the floor leads into some blackness below.
name: Beneath the Stalactites

desc:
The damp walls of this crooked tunnel have seemingly been carved by crude tools
into a canvas of sorts, bearing primitive drawings formed of shallow scratches
in the rock beneath. Most of them depict multiple robust humanoids fighting each
other or feasting on animals and smaller humanoids alike.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
Complete darkness reigns supreme within these mountains. This great cavern seems
have been roughly cut into the mountain. Crude, dark murals span the walls,
depicting scenes of violence and debauchery. They appear to be painted with a
strangely red, viscous liquid.
name: Troll Hall

desc:
The moist, mould-covered walls enclose a small, roughly square-shaped burrow.
The nearly-decayed remainders of a few wooden barrels lie next to a pile of
ripped cloth and bone fragments, all stained with a dark, reddish-brown crust of
unknown origin.
name: Twisted Burrow

desc:
The floor and walls are damp and slippery in this roughly rotund cavern section.
Tiny streams of water trickle in from various fissures in the walls, forming
brown, algae-filled puddles of various sizes all across the uneven floor. Even
though most of this cavern seems to be of natural origin, a few larger fissures
in the walls have definitely been widened after the fact.
name: Subterranean Intersection

desc:
An overpowering odour rifles through the air, stripping it of any semblance of
nature. Great piles of gnawed bones litter the floor, with one particularly
massive heap laying claim to the centre of the room. This place wears death like
a heavy cloak. Occasionally, the bones seem to move, as if something was
crawling under the surface.
name: Bone Heap

desc:
Considerably warmer than the neighbouring cavern, this cave abruptly leads to a
dead end. Piles of charred stones are scattered here and there bleeding a
choking smoke from beneath. It is difficult to see through the haze and the
scent of burning cracked rock fills the nostrils.
name: Smoky Cave

desc:
The hue of the rock here turns to a greyish yellow colour. Great spires of stone
strike upwards and downwards from the ceiling and floor. Pools of water cover
the ground allowing for little movement save on a path that leads west. The
shallow water glows with a faint, iridescent green light.
name: Stalagmite Cavern

desc:
A cold wind scorches this steep ridge, and continuing the ascent south leads up
to a high mountain peak covered in mist. Further south, lower slopes can be
glimpsed beyond the peak. There is no level ground to be seen anywhere near as
boulders dot the mountain side like barnacles on a stone.
name: Steep Mountain Slope

desc:
This is high on the slopes of one of the taller peaks of a mountain spur, and
the only direction from here is down, whether by choice, or by a most untimely
fall. The way down seems most unstable, and it is evident that anything that
descends from here will take some of the mountain with it.
name: Cairn on a High Peak

desc:
Towering peaks loom high above to the south and east. The cracked and uneven
ground is littered with loose rocks and large boulders half buried in dirt, many
of which must have fallen here from the higher slopes. A chill wind constantly
buffets the mountainside, suggesting that this may not be a wise place to
linger.
name: Mountain Slope

desc:
A narrow, rock ledge curves along the base of some high mountain cliffs here,
offering a means of travel between the rocky crags that extend to the south, and
a steep, but scalable ascent to the west. The snow-capped peaks that reach high
into the sky to the north and east can barely be seen from this vantage point.
name: Narrow, Rocky Traverse

desc:
This mountain soars above the rest, only rising to greater heights to the east.
A basin has formed here containing a frozen lake, and to the east an icy path
can be discerned climbing its way further up the mountain. Winds carrying ice
and snow flurry fiercely, while voices in the air mutter and scream endlessly.
name: Atop a Whispering Mountain

desc:
Rocky cliffs and mountain slopes abound to the east, their lofty, snow-clad
peaks reaching high into the cloudy skies. The southern cliffs are sheer and
slippery. Gentler inclines to the east and west offer a choice of routes from
here, though in all cases the journey would not be easy, the uneven, always
sloping ground strewn with loose rocks and boulders.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
A wide ravine opens up to the north, spanned by a decrepit bridge. East and
west, the mountain peaks stretch out in a long chain that reaches far into the
distance. To the southeast the still higher peaks of the Mountains of Moria
reach far up into the skies, their heads shrouded in great, grey, billowing
clouds. Imposing cliffs loom above to the south and west, an impenetrable
barrier to travel. Lesser slopes, that are strewn with boulders, but otherwise
passable, extend to the north and east.
name: In the Mountains

desc:
The bridge spans a deep, rocky ravine. Built long ago by an unknown hand, it is
now so time-worn as to be dilapidated. Flat and without any form of hand rails,
the bridge spans the chasm as the wind screams across it. The stonework has been
sanded smooth by the incessant, torturing wind.
name: Weathered Narrow Bridge

desc:
The mountain slope descends toward a high cliff to the north, whilst a taller
peak looms above to the west. To the south, the mountainside is interrupted by a
chasm. There is little protection here from the biting wind that blows through
the mountains from the east, and the stones around are worn smooth and
featureless from the strong wind.
name: Mountain Slope

desc:
The mountain slope is speckled with boulders, but for the most part the ground
here is fairly even. The cliff to the north offers a view of the lands far
below, though from this height nothing can be discerned in detail. East and
west, this slope reaches along the spur, with a tall, towering mountain standing
above the rest and barring passage to the south. Many large boulders lie upon
the ground here, split open by the cruel weather, or shattered as they have
fallen from the higher slopes.
name: Along a Mountain Slope

desc:
Riding is out of the question here. No discernible pathway can be seen along
these rocky slopes, so the only option is to pick a way between the rocks and
boulders, seeking out the most level ground as best is possible. Mighty peaks
loom high above, their crests hidden from sight amid the billowing clouds.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
A low mountain range cuts through the area. The air that rolls down from those
heights is frigid, and chilling to the bone. And the elements have taken their
toll on the landscape itself, with no noticeable vegetation growing here at all.
Rocks and boulders are all that occupies these lonely slopes.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
A dark crack leads down into a web-draped grotto. A slight draft can be felt,
hinting that the outdoors are somewhere nearby to the north. Webs have been
heavily spun across the grotto, rarely hampering movement, but giving a spooky
ambience as they billow in the draft of the cave.
name: Web-draped Grotto

desc:
As this path winds its way from the mountains to the northeast down to the wide
plains to the west, the width of the trail thins a bit, perhaps to prevent it
from being seen from afar. The path carefully follows the river that flows to
the south; never far from a source of water, and a place to hide one's tracks.
name: A Secret Mountain Path

desc:
Following a quiet stream to the southeast, this trail is hardly discernible,
even while being travelled. Whoever first created this path was careful to pass
around any obstructions, rather than moving them or going over; at most times,
the trail goes to the north of any obstacles, so the path is shaded even when
the sun is directly overhead.
name: A Secret Trail

desc:
Following the bend in the stream to the south and east, this trail makes its way
north and west alongside great distances of plains. A steady wind sweeps the
ground at all times, polishing stones and exposing the cracked soil.
name: A Secret Trail

desc:
Passing from east to west along the stream to the south, this faint trail is
sometimes hidden from view beneath the heavy silt that blankets the area; at
times it seems to simply vanish. Plains extend out of sight to the north.
name: A Faint Trail

desc:
Constant winds drift over this area, rippling the surface of the water that
passes to the south, and wiping this place clean of tracks, given time. The path
itself is always at risk of becoming buried as bits of soil are blown into its
path, but for now it remains faintly visible below.
name: A Faint Trail

desc:
Making a gentle bend from the south to the east as it tries to follow a stream
through the area, the nature of this trail is not very clear. It is faint enough
to be an animal trail, yet deliberate and carefully placed.
name: A Faint Trail

desc:
A small ring of stones, with even smaller bits of coal and unburned wood, are
all that suggest that this place may be a campsite. Located just west of a
flowing stream, which should be suitable for drinking, this site seems to be the
terminal end of a trail that leads north. No other signs of road or civilisation
can be seen, making this place even more odd.
name: A Small Camp

desc:
In the pitch black water it is very difficult to see. It's quite impossible to
guess how deep it goes downward; into some deep fissure where dark, ancient
things lurk most likely. There is no space between the rock above the water for
a breath of air. To the east, none would ever call light, but the darkness seems
less absolute.
name: Submerged Cave

desc:
Black waves lap against a stony beach making no sound whatsoever. To the west
the cavern wall comes down to the water and dips below, disappearing from sight.
The beach is quite small, only a few yards across, but a path of freshly wet
rock winds its way out of sight upwards.
name: Shore of an Underground Lake

desc:
This chamber is dark in all corners but beads of condensation scatter the dim
light as it drips down the walls of this damp cavern. Small bones are decaying
in scattered piles throughout the floor. Several large nests of fur and hay have
been arranged around the chamber for something to bed down.
name: Troll's Home

desc:
A hovel has been roughly made with fish skins draped across some upright stones.
Beneath the "shelter" the bones of dead fish litter the area, and near the back
the ground appears very smooth, as if large fists have pounded it into something
more akin to a bed than the cave floor. A rough picture has been scraped into a
large flat stone in the northern part of the cave.
name: Hovel within the Rock

desc:
A pool of water, bubbling and rippling as if almost at a boil, dominates the
centre of this cavern. The dark walls of the cave are stained an even darker hue
than usual due to the moisture in the air. Along the south wall a low ledge
seems to have been hewn into the surface like a crude shelf.
name: Steam-Filled Cave
